name: Digital River Global Commerce API
what_it_does: Global commerce and merchant of record platform with REST API — enabling software, gaming, and digital goods companies to sell globally with Digital River acting as the legal seller of record, handling payment processing, global tax collection (VAT/GST/sales tax), compliance, fraud prevention, and currency conversion across 240 countries and territories.
use_cases: Acting as merchant of record via Digital River API for global digital goods tax compliance
Processing global payments via Digital River for 240+ countries and territories
Managing product catalogs and pricing via Digital River API for global software distribution
Handling subscription lifecycle via Digital River for recurring digital goods billing
Integrating checkout via Digital River API for embedded commerce in games and software
not_for: Physical goods with complex logistics/fulfillment (Digital River is digital goods focused)
Simple US-only e-commerce without global compliance needs (use Stripe for simplicity)
Small businesses without enterprise-scale transaction volumes
best_when: A large software, gaming, or digital goods company needs to sell in 240+ countries with full merchant of record services — where Digital River eliminates global tax liability, compliance risk, and payment infrastructure complexity for enterprise-scale digital commerce.
avoid_when: You're a startup or SMB, sell physical goods, or don't need global merchant of record compliance services.

auth.notes: API key via Authorization header (Bearer format). Public key for client-side operations (checkout). Secret key for server-side API calls. Separate test and live key pairs. Webhooks with signature validation (X-DR-Signature). JS SDK for drop-in checkout. Java, .NET, PHP server-side clients. No OAuth2. Enterprise accounts managed via Digital River portal.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; sandbox/test environment for development
pricing.paid_tiers: Revenue share model; Enterprise custom pricing based on GMV
pricing.notes: Eden Prairie, Minnesota. Founded 1994. Raised/acquired multiple times — private equity owned (Francisco Partners). One of the original e-commerce platforms for software distribution. 240+ countries. 175+ currencies. 50+ payment methods. Handles: EU VAT, UK VAT, Australian GST, Canadian GST, US sales tax, digital services tax. Customers: Samsung, Canon, Logitech, HP, 2K Games, Square Enix, BitDefender. Gaming focus: in-game purchases, DLC, subscription. Fraud prevention built-in. Chargeback protection. Compliance: PCI-DSS Level 1, GDPR, CCPA, SOC2. IPO filed and withdrawn multiple times.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with Bearer API key. Public (client) vs secret (server) key pair. Webhooks with signature validation. JS/Java/.NET/PHP SDKs. PCI-DSS Level 1/GDPR/CCPA/SOC2 compliant. 240+ countries. Enterprise-only — annual contract. Good modern API documentation.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Order creation idempotent with idempotency key. Checkout session reads idempotent by ID. Payment capture — verify state before retry. Subscription reads idempotent by subscription ID.
known_agent_gotchas: Two key types: public key (client-side checkout) and secret key (server-side API) — never expose secret key
Merchant of Record model: Digital River is legal seller of record, not your company — impacts tax reporting
Enterprise contract required — not available for small merchants or self-service signup
Webhook signature validation mandatory — verify X-DR-Signature to prevent spoofing
Test mode uses test keys — live keys require production account approval
240-country coverage includes complex compliance — API handles it but understand the MoR implications

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. PCI-DSS Level 1 certified. GDPR/CCPA/SOC2. Public vs secret key separation. Webhook signature validation. Payment data handled by Digital River (MoR) — reduces PCI scope for merchants. US and EU data residency. Enterprise security review. Fraud prevention built-in.
